news.title: From Claims Control to Operational Governance
news.summary: In many insurance lines, periods of stress follow a familiar pattern: deteriorating experience prompts corrective action, which alters behavior, and those behavioral changes further destabilize results. At times, actuaries recognize this dynamic in hindsight, after loss ratios, lapses or utilization trends have already exceeded expectations. In the post-pandemic environment, this pattern has become more pronounced […
news.whyItMatters: Relevant to claims trend, pricing, reserving, loss ratio analysis, and forecast assumptions.

news.title: Curiosity and Honesty
news.summary: In recognition of Asian American, Native Hawaiian and Pacific Islander (AANHPI) Heritage Month, I connected with Jon Wu, FSA, MAAA, CERA, to gain his insights into an actuarial career guided by curiosity, honesty and a commitment to always helping others. Here, Jon, who retired in 2024 from his consulting practice that focused on enterprise risk management […] The post Curiosity and Honesty appeared first on The Actu
news.whyItMatters: Relevant to actuarial career awareness, market terminology, analytics skills, and interview talking points.

news.title: From Pension Risk to Alignment
news.summary: As reports and articles detail, Canada’s retirement system is evolving, driven by shifting demographics, market conditions, and longer lifespans, all of which are reshaping what retirement looks like and how Canadians and their employers prepare for it. In this environment, innovation in plan design, investment strategy and plan management is a key pillar that helps […] The post From Pension Risk to Alignment appeare
news.whyItMatters: Relevant to actuarial career awareness, market terminology, analytics skills, and interview talking points.

news.title: Leading Through Change
news.summary: Editor’s note: Society of Actuaries (SOA) CEO Greg Heidrich originally presented some of this content about the changing industry landscape during the 2026 ReFocus Conference, which was sponsored by the SOA and the American Council of Life Insurers (ACLI). We live in a world of great change that is often disorienting, confusing and at times […] The post Leading Through Change appeared first on The Actuary Magazine .

jobSearchLinks.whatToWatch: Prioritize postings mentioning claims trend, pricing, rate filing, risk adjustment, ACA, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, SQL, and dashboarding.
Prioritize postings mentioning valuation, reserves, annuities, asset adequacy, reinsurance, mortality, assumption governance, and model validation.
This is likely the easiest transition title for a Python/SQL/data-science profile. Watch for insurance data, reporting automation, and model support tasks.
Watch how job posts describe pricing support, reserve movement, loss ratio, experience study, and management reporting.

sources.name: NAIC Life Actuarial Task Force
sources.use: Life valuation, annuity assumptions, reinsurance, asset adequacy, complex assets, and regulatory development.

sources.name: NAIC Health Actuarial Task Force
sources.use: Health actuarial regulation, rate review, claims trend, Medicare/Medicaid, and risk adjustment issues.

sources.use: Policy-facing actuarial updates, public statements, and practice-area commentary.

sources.use: Health practice notes, issue briefs, and health insurance policy topics.

sources.use: Life insurance, annuity, longevity, valuation, and policy topics.

sources.use: SOA research, profession updates, and actuarial industry announcements.

sources.use: General actuarial profession articles, technology, emerging risks, and practice trends.
